div.span: i care a lot about the things i make
i spent hours on OBS overlays i never go live with
works on my machine. also works on the server. rare.
i don't stop because i'm done. i stop because i'm tired.
that feeling when the e-ink refreshes exactly right
i think about edge cases in the shower
i test things. sometimes before shipping.
open hardware should be for everyone
custom animated OBS scenes. for an audience of zero.
the first version was worse. that's the point.
maker. engineer. whatever it takes.
i like projects with sharp edges
hardware nerd with a GitHub account
started simple. added docker. you know how it goes.
german engineering. self-inflicted.
i automate things i could just do manually. it's a gift.
deleted more code than i wrote today. productive.
built a full streaming setup. purely for vibes.
i have a bambu lab x1c and a problem with starting new projects
wanted to check departures. ended up writing a captive portal.
closed laptop. opened it again. just one more thing.
still learning. always building.
if something annoyed me once, i'll fix it so it never does again
it started as a keychain. it now has a docker backend.
my commit history tells the real story
one button on my stream deck deploys crewvolution to prod
yes those OBS scenes have custom animated backgrounds. i made them.
the readme is longer than the actual project
once saw a problem at an event. now it's a SaaS.
it's 2am and the firmware finally deep-sleeps correctly
i have custom OBS overlays for coding, electronics, and gaming
always building something
i have too many open tabs and half-finished PCBs
i told myself i'd keep it simple this time
i have more projects than hours in the day. working on both.
